---
title: "Groupes de travail SIASP"
subtitle: "Campagne SIASP 2024"
institute: Insee
author: "Léna Esbelin, Lino Galiana, Téo Moreau"
date: "Novembre-Décembre 2025"
description: |
  GT par versant
slide-tone: false # for blind readers
chalkboard: false # press the B key to toggle chalkboard
jump-to-slide: true #press "g" to jump and the number of slide
mouse-wheel: false
transition: slide
fig-cap-location: bottom

embed-resources: false

custom-toc: true
execute: 
  cache: true
---

## Résultat principal

```{python}
import polars as pl
from main import import_fig1
import polars.selectors as cs
from great_tables import *
from plotnine import *

fig1_eff = import_fig1()

(
    GT(
        fig1_eff
    )
    .fmt_percent(
        cs.contains(["evol"]),
        scale_values=False
    )
    .fmt_number(
        cs.contains(["2023", "2024"]),
        decimals = 0,
        sep_mark = " "
    )
    .fmt_nanoplot(columns="2024_yca_bar", plot_type="bar")
    .fmt_nanoplot(columns="evol_yca_bar", plot_type="bar")
    .tab_style(
      style=style.fill(color="#c5eceb"),
      locations=loc.body(
          rows = (pl.col("versant").str.contains("Ensemble de la fonction publique"))
      )
   )
)
```

```{python}
ojs_define(fig1_eff = fig1_eff.to_pandas())
```

```{ojs}
fig1_df = transpose(fig1_eff)
```

```{ojs}
Plot.plot({
  height: 420,
  x: {
    label: "Versant",
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.waffleY(fig1_df, {
      x: "versant",
      y: "2024_yca",
      fill: "versant",
      unit: 100,
      tip: true,
      /*rx: "100%"*/
    })
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
Plot = require("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6.17/dist/plot.umd.min.js")
```
